title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  1 -  2017: Castles of England for Google Earth

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog at 2:31 AM on December 2, 2017, updating on his Google Earth castles project. He explains he is finishing adding England/Britain castle images sourced from Wikipedia and hopes to complete castles by December 15th before moving on to cathedrals. He also mentions listening to Plato's Laws and Aristotle's Politics.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: excited about its potential and wants to make videos showcasing the detailed 3D castle models
believes ancient Greeks like Plato and Aristotle were more educated than people are today
wants to finish castles by December 15th then move on to cathedrals, though acknowledges timeline pressure

transcript.text: Hello, Living in the Boulder Vlog. It's 2.31am on December 2nd on Saturday, 2017. So this
Will be for the 1st of December. I haven't done one since a while. I can't remember when,
But I've been too busy. Last weekend we went down to visit my brother's house. He got a
House in Ridgeway. It's a nice place. Reminds me of the 1800s. I only managed to do maybe
Like 20 minutes or something of work on my website while I was down there. I did some
Images doing Google Earth of the castles. I can't remember where I was the last time
I did the video, but I was trying to power through all the castles. So yeah, right now
I finished all of them except for Britain. But Britain was the only one where I hadn't
Done anything. And so now I'm collecting photos from Wikipedia of all the castles. I got a
Little bit of a head start. I collected KMZs of a bunch of the castles. And so I copied
Them and I have them located. So whenever I see them I can just search and it will already
Go there. It saves a little bit of time. So yeah. So maybe I could finish all of Britain
Tomorrow. And I have to do some stuff on Sunday. But yeah. I think my original goal was to finish
Castles and cathedrals by December 15th. And so that gives me like, you know, if I finish
Castles tomorrow, that means it gives me like two weeks to do cathedrals. And I already have the
Images of them. So I'm easy to find. So I'm on schedule. But then I think instead of linking them to my
Timeline. So that's what I wanted to do before. I think the gravitational pull of Google Earth
In Rome will be too strong. And that's where I will depart from my segue into my timeline. But that
Was a good, solid push. And it's a great KMZ file to have all the different stuff that I collected, like the
Castles. I'll put them on a link for people to download. And yeah, eventually I want to make
Movies, YouTube videos, showing like, you know, most of these castles are already built up on Google
Earth. It's a cool thing, like a cartoon with detail. It's amazing going them and stuff in the
The courtyard. And even the railways are done and flags. It's really cool. But yeah, I want to make
Videos. And both the ones that they don't have them, I could build my own 3D with their walls in
The castle and do videos of the trips that they did from castle to castle and the interactions they
Have and specific battles they have for YouTube. That'll be fun. I can do them all by request because
There's so many of them. But yeah, that's going to be super amazing to finish around. But I don't
Know how much work that'll be. We'll see. So I've been listening to Plato and Aristotle Plato,
Laws and Aristotle politics, trying to memorize all the stuff they had to say about legal stories and
Structures that they had back then. I like only listening to the classics but back in those days
Because it makes me feel like I live in this world, in that world, more than this world. Like
The way that they think and everything. They were really educated back then. Like maybe more educated
Than we are now. I don't know. Do you have a video about that maybe sometime? All right,
I think I've talked long enough. My tongue is dry. Okay, peace out.
